rationale: The page provides detailed information on Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) financing in India, the role of the 15th Finance Commission, and the UNDRR. It also introduces the C-FLOOD platform for flood forecasting, which are key topics in environmental disaster management.
cleanedText:
5.2. DISASTER RISK REDUCTION (DRR) FINANCING
Why in the News?
United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction (UNDRR) released the Global Assessment Report (GAR) titled ‐Resilience Pays: Financing and Investing for our Future‑.
India’s Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) Financing System
• DRR Finance Mechanism: India follows pre-determined, rule-based allocations from national to state and district levels, supported by Disaster Management Act of 2005.
• Current Allocations: Under 15th Finance Commission, it exceeds INR 2.32 trillion (~USD 28 billion).
• New Methodology: 15th Finance Commission’s new methodology for state-wise allocations, replaces the earlier expenditure driven methodology.
o It combines capacity, risk exposure and proneness to hazard & vulnerability (Disaster Risk Index).
• Disaster Mitigation Funds: 15th Finance Commission recommended creation of National Disaster Mitigation Fund (NDMF) and State Disaster Mitigation Fund (SDMF).
o Centre contributes 75% for all States (90% for North-Eastern/Himalayan States) in SDMF.
• Global Leadership: India showcased World’s Largest Disaster Risk Reduction Financing System at 8th session of Global Platform for Disaster Risk Reduction (GPDRR, 2025).
o GPDRR was established in 2006 to assess implementation of Sendai Framework for DRR (2015-30).
o Sendai Framework is successor to Hyogo Framework for Action (HFA) 2005-2015, adopted at 3rd United Nations Conference on DRR (2015), in Sendai Japan.
[Box]: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction (UNDRR)
• Location: Geneva, Switzerland
• Genesis: Originally established as UNISDR in 1999 to facilitate an International Strategy for Disaster Reduction (ISDR).
• About: Lead agency within UN system for the coordination of DRR.
• Mission: Provide leadership and support to accelerate global efforts in disaster risk reduction to achieve inclusive sustainable development and the goal of the Sendai Framework (2015-30).
5.3. C-FLOOD
Why in the News?
Recently inaugurated by Union Minister of Jal Shakti.
About C-FLOOD
• A Web-based platform providing 2-day advance flood inundation forecasts up to village-level.
o Uses advanced 2-D hydrodynamic modelling.
o Offers flood inundation maps and water level predictions to aid disaster preparedness.
• Significance: Unified system integrating flood modelling outputs from national and regional agencies.
• Jointly developed by:
o Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (C-DAC), Pune.
o Central Water Commission (CWC).
o Department of Water Resources, River Development & Ganga Rejuvenation, Ministry of Jal Shakti.
o National Remote Sensing Centre (NRSC).
[Map]: INDIA FLOOD HAZARD ZONES
The map displays areas liable to flood across India, highlighting major river systems including the Indus, Ganga, Brahmaputra, Narmada, Tapi, Godavari, Krishna, and Mahanadi basins.
